content.text: SUM_SSC is responsible for the following functions

In the below example, Signal A,B,C and D are protected signals, so instead of SWC directly passing the signal value to COM, it will send to SSC. SSC will add authentication to the payload and then transmit to COM
In case of signal E which is unprotected, SWC can directly send to COM
Aggregator of signals that compose the Transmit Data Region of an Authenticated Message Trigger the generation of the Alive Rolling Count (ARC), Protection Value (PV), CheckSum(CS) and/or CRC

items: Generation of the Authentication Region of an Authenticated Message
Update the Authoritative Counter (Tx confirmation)
Transmit the Authoritative Counter in the Counter Sync Message
Error handling / storage
Ensure synchronization of Authoritative Counter value with regards to periodic message transmission

content.text: Transmitters of Non-Authenticated Messages having an ARC, PV, CS and/or CRC:

items: Aggregator of the data signals that compose the Message
Trigger the generation of the Alive Rolling Count (ARC), Protection Value (PV), CheckSum(CS) and/or CR
Verification of the Authentication Region of an Authenticated Message
Receive the Authoritative Counter from the Counter Sync Message
Maintain a Tentative Counter
Update / Manage the Active Counter locally
Update / Manage the MAC Pass Counter
Update / Manage the MAC Fail Counter
Transmission of the signal(s) in the Transmit Data Region of a verified Authenticated
Passes the ARC, PV, CS and/or CRC for checking and status notification
Message to the appropriate functional SW-C(s)
Error handling / storage

content.text: Receivers of Non-Authenticated Messages having an ARC, PV, CS and/or CRC:

items: Passes the ARC, PV, CS and/or CRC for checking and status notification
Message to the appropriate functional SW-C(s)
Processing of unique Seed / Key Challenge Response requests (based on security levels supported by ECU) received from Diagnostics modules
Random number seed generation
Use of Software Crypto Library or Crypto HW to support Seed/Key access based on ECU H/W requirements
Verification of Seed /Key Challenge response
Generation of Security Peripheral Memory Update Messages
Manufacturing First Time Key Update
Provision of Security Peripheral General Keys
Error detection associated with Key Provisioning
